events.title: Creation
events.description: God creates the heavens, earth, and humanity in six days. Adam and Eve dwell in the Garden of Eden.
events.significance: The foundation of all reality; establishes the Creator-creature relationship.

events.title: The Fall
events.description: Adam and Eve disobey God, eating the forbidden fruit. Sin and death enter creation. God promises a Redeemer (3:15).
events.significance: The wound that the rest of the Bible heals.

events.title: Cain Murders Abel
events.description: The first murder: Cain kills his brother Abel out of jealousy. God places a mark on Cain.
events.significance: Sin's spread in the first generation; Abel's blood cries out foreshadowing the Lamb.

events.title: Enoch Walks with God
events.description: Enoch walked with God for 365 years and 'was not, for God took him' — he never died.
events.significance: A preview of resurrection and translation; Hebrews 11:5.

events.title: Noah and the Flood
events.description: Global flood judges humanity's wickedness. Noah, his family, and animals are saved in the ark. The rainbow covenant is given.
events.significance: Judgment and salvation; a type of baptism (1 Peter 3:20–21) and eschatological judgment.

events.title: Tower of Babel
events.description: Humanity unites to build a tower to heaven. God confuses their language and scatters them across the earth.
events.significance: Origin of the nations; reversed at Pentecost (Acts 2) when all hear in their own language.

events.title: Abraham's Call
events.description: God calls Abram from Ur to leave for a land he would be shown. God makes three promises: land, seed, and blessing.
events.significance: The pivot of the OT; the Abrahamic covenant drives the rest of the biblical story.

events.title: Sodom and Gomorrah
events.description: God destroys Sodom and Gomorrah with fire and brimstone for their wickedness. Lot is rescued.
events.significance: A warning of final judgment; Jude 7 calls it 'an example of eternal fire.'

events.title: Isaac Born & Offered
events.description: The child of promise is born to 100-year-old Abraham and 90-year-old Sarah. Abraham then offers Isaac on Moriah.
events.significance: Isaac's 'resurrection' is the greatest OT preview of Christ's resurrection (Hebrews 11:17–19).

events.title: Jacob and the 12 Tribes
events.description: Jacob fathers twelve sons who become the twelve tribes of Israel. He wrestles with God and is renamed Israel.
events.significance: The twelve tribes are the nation through which the Messiah will come.

events.title: Joseph in Egypt
events.description: Joseph is sold by his brothers, becomes Pharaoh's right hand, and saves Egypt and his family from famine.
events.significance: Greatest OT type of Christ: betrayed, exalted, brings salvation to his people.

events.title: Moses Born
events.description: Moses is born to Levite parents during Pharaoh's genocide of Hebrew boys. He is hidden in a basket on the Nile.
events.significance: Foreshadows Jesus's infancy: a powerful king tries to kill the deliverer-child.

events.title: The Burning Bush
events.description: God appears to Moses in a burning bush and reveals His name: I AM. Moses is commissioned to deliver Israel.
events.significance: The first full revelation of God's covenant name (YHWH); Jesus will claim 'I AM' in John's Gospel.

events.title: The Exodus
events.description: Ten plagues culminate in the Passover. Israel escapes Egypt. The Red Sea parts; Israel crosses on dry ground.
events.significance: The defining redemption event of the OT; a type of salvation from sin's bondage.

events.title: Law Given at Sinai
events.description: God descends on Mount Sinai in fire and smoke. He gives Moses the Ten Commandments and the whole Mosaic Law.
events.significance: The Mosaic Covenant; reveals sin's seriousness and points to the need for Christ.

events.title: Tabernacle Constructed
events.description: God gives detailed instructions for the portable sanctuary. The glory cloud fills the completed Tabernacle.
events.significance: God dwelling among His people; every element foreshadows Christ.

events.title: Israel Enters Canaan
events.description: After 40 years in the wilderness, Israel crosses the Jordan under Joshua and begins to take the Promised Land.
events.significance: Joshua (same name as Jesus in Hebrew) leads Israel into rest — a picture of Christ.

events.title: Period of the Judges
events.description: A cycle of apostasy, oppression, crying out, and deliverance through judges. 'Everyone did what was right in their own eyes.'
events.significance: Shows human inability to keep the covenant; points to the need for an eternal King.

events.title: Ruth and Boaz
events.description: Ruth, a Moabite widow, finds kinsman-redeemer Boaz in Bethlehem. They marry and become great-grandparents of David.
events.significance: Greatest OT picture of redemption; Boaz is a type of Christ the kinsman-redeemer.

events.title: Samuel and Saul
events.description: Israel demands a king. God gives them Saul, a tall man from Benjamin. He disobeys and is rejected by God.
events.significance: Human kingship fails; it takes a man after God's own heart to bring peace.

events.title: David Anointed King
events.description: David the shepherd boy is anointed by Samuel. After years of exile, he becomes king over all Israel. He conquers Jerusalem.
events.significance: The Davidic covenant (2 Samuel 7) establishes the messianic lineage.

events.title: Solomon's Temple Built
events.description: Solomon builds the Temple in Jerusalem over seven years. The glory of God fills it at the dedication.
events.significance: The permanent dwelling of God's presence; points to the ultimate temple — Jesus and the New Jerusalem.

events.title: Kingdom Divides
events.description: After Solomon's death, the kingdom splits: Israel (10 northern tribes) and Judah (2 southern tribes). Jeroboam leads the north into idolatry.
events.significance: The beginning of the long road to exile; the prophetic period intensifies.

events.title: Elijah and the Prophets
events.description: Elijah confronts the Baal-worshipping king Ahab. Fire falls at Carmel. He is taken to heaven in a chariot of fire.
events.significance: Elijah is the promised forerunner of the Messiah (Malachi 4:5); his 'return' is fulfilled in John the Baptist.

events.title: Isaiah's Ministry
events.description: Isaiah prophesies for 60 years under four kings. He sees the throne of God and gives the most detailed messianic prophecies in the OT.
events.significance: Isaiah 53 describes the suffering servant with such precision it has been called 'the fifth Gospel.'

events.title: Northern Kingdom Falls
events.description: Assyria under Shalmaneser V destroys Samaria. The ten northern tribes are deported and scattered — the 'lost tribes of Israel.'
events.significance: Judgment on persistent idolatry; the ten tribes are largely absorbed into the nations.

events.title: Josiah's Reformation
events.description: Young King Josiah discovers the Book of the Law. He tears his robes, cleanses the Temple, and leads the greatest revival in Judah's history.
events.significance: The last great revival before the Babylonian exile; shows that even revival cannot permanently reverse the covenant curse.

events.title: Babylon Conquers Judah
events.description: Nebuchadnezzar defeats Judah in three waves (605, 597, 586 BC). Jerusalem and the Temple are destroyed. The people are exiled to Babylon.
events.significance: The Davidic throne appears extinguished — making the messianic promises even more miraculous.

events.title: Daniel in Babylon
events.description: Daniel and friends remain faithful in exile. Daniel interprets dreams and receives visions of four world empires and the Son of Man.
events.significance: Daniel 9:25–26 gives the precise timetable for the Messiah's coming and death.

events.title: Ezekiel's Visions
events.description: Ezekiel prophesies to the exiles. He sees the glory departing the Temple, the valley of dry bones, and the future Temple.
events.significance: Ezekiel 37 (dry bones) and 36:26–27 (new heart and spirit) point to the New Covenant and resurrection.

events.title: Cyrus Decrees Return
events.description: Persian King Cyrus issues a decree allowing the Jews to return to Israel and rebuild the Temple — named by Isaiah 150 years earlier!
events.significance: Confirms the precision of biblical prophecy; the first wave returns under Zerubbabel.

events.title: Temple Rebuilt
events.description: After opposition and delay, the Second Temple is completed under Zerubbabel. The older priests weep, remembering Solomon's glory.
events.significance: Haggai 2:9 promises 'the glory of this latter house shall be greater' — fulfilled when Jesus enters it.

events.title: Ezra and Nehemiah
events.description: Ezra leads a spiritual revival; Nehemiah rebuilds Jerusalem's walls in 52 days despite opposition.
events.significance: The decree of Nehemiah 2 is the starting point for Daniel's 70-weeks calculation.

events.title: Malachi — Last Prophet
events.description: The last OT prophet. Malachi rebukes Israel's half-hearted worship and promises a forerunner before 'the great day of the LORD.'
events.significance: Closes the OT. After this, 400 years of prophetic silence before John the Baptist's voice.

events.title: 400 Years of Silence
events.description: No new prophets arise. Israel suffers under Greek and then Roman rule. The Pharisees and Sadducees emerge.
events.significance: The silence intensifies expectation for the Messiah. The world stage is set: Roman peace, Greek language, Jewish diaspora.

events.title: Birth of John the Baptist
events.description: Gabriel announces to Zechariah that his barren wife Elizabeth will bear a son named John — the forerunner of the Lord.
events.significance: Breaks the 400-year silence; the final OT-style prophet who bridges the two Testaments.

events.title: Birth of Jesus
events.description: Jesus is born in Bethlehem to Mary, a virgin, during the census of Augustus. Angels announce to shepherds; Magi bring gifts.
events.significance: The Incarnation: God becomes man. The Word made flesh (John 1:14).

events.title: Baptism of Jesus
events.description: Jesus is baptised by John in the Jordan. The Spirit descends as a dove; the Father speaks: 'This is my beloved Son.'
events.significance: The inauguration of Jesus's ministry; the Trinity is revealed; fulfils Isaiah 42:1.

events.title: Temptation in the Desert
events.description: The Spirit leads Jesus into the wilderness for 40 days. Satan tests Him three times; Jesus rebuffs each with Scripture.
events.significance: Where Israel failed 40 years in the wilderness, the Second Adam succeeds.

events.title: Sermon on the Mount
events.description: Jesus delivers the magna carta of the Kingdom of God: Beatitudes, deeper law, prayer, and the two ways.
events.significance: Jesus interprets the Law from the inside — as the New Moses giving a new Torah.

events.title: Triumphal Entry
events.description: Jesus enters Jerusalem on a donkey to crowds shouting Hosanna. Palm branches are laid in His path.
events.significance: Fulfils Zechariah 9:9 precisely; announces His Kingship.

events.title: The Last Supper
events.description: Jesus eats the Passover with His disciples. He institutes the Lord's Supper, inaugurating the New Covenant with His blood.
events.significance: The Passover Lamb and the New Covenant come together in one meal.

events.title: Crucifixion
events.description: Jesus is crucified at Golgotha. The veil of the Temple tears. Darkness for three hours. 'It is finished.'
events.significance: The Suffering Servant prophecy (Isaiah 53) fulfilled in every detail; sins atoned for.

events.title: Resurrection
events.description: On the third day Jesus rises bodily from the tomb. He appears to Mary, the disciples, and over 500 people.
events.significance: Vindicates Jesus's claims, defeats death, and guarantees the resurrection of believers.

events.title: Ascension
events.description: Forty days after the resurrection, Jesus ascends bodily into heaven before witnesses.
events.significance: Takes His seat at the Father's right hand (Psalm 110:1); intercedes for believers.

events.title: Pentecost
events.description: The Holy Spirit descends on 120 disciples with fire and wind. 3,000 are baptised. The Church is born.
events.significance: Reversal of Babel; the promised Spirit poured out; Church age begins.

events.title: Paul's Conversion
events.description: Saul of Tarsus, persecutor of Christians, is blinded by the risen Christ on the road to Damascus and converts.
events.significance: The most dramatic conversion in history; Paul becomes the apostle to the Gentiles.

events.title: Jerusalem Destroyed
events.description: Roman General Titus besieges and destroys Jerusalem and the Temple, as Jesus predicted in precise detail.
events.significance: Fulfils Jesus's prophecy; the Mosaic age ends; Jewish worship is radically altered.

events.title: Revelation Written
events.description: The aged apostle John, exiled on Patmos, receives the final vision — the Revelation of Jesus Christ.
events.significance: Completes the biblical canon; shows the triumph of Christ and the New Creation.
